title: The étale fundamental group of a smooth variety in characteristic p>0 and its relation to stratifications
speaker: Hélène Esnault
tags: Workshop on Fundamental Groups and Periods

proof_stages.label: Stratifications, crystals, and F-divided sheaves
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines coherent infinitesimal crystals and F-divided sheaves in characteristic p, records their Tannakian nature, and explains the Bhatt/Saavedra equivalence and Cartier-descent identification with coherent D-modules in the smooth case.

proof_stages.label: Tannakian fundamental groups and motivating questions
proof_stages.summary: For algebraically closed k the profinite completion of the stratified/crystalline Tannaka group is the étale fundamental group, and the proper Esnault-Mehta theorem motivates asking whether trivial étale fundamental group forces trivial stratifications in the nonproper case.

proof_stages.label: Nonproper theorem under codimension-two boundary
proof_stages.summary: The central theorem is stated: for k equal to the algebraic closure of a finite field, if X is the regular locus of a normal projective variety with complement of codimension at least two and π1^ét(X) is trivial, then the infinitesimal-crystal/stratified fundamental group is trivial.

proof_stages.label: Boundedness for Frobenius-divided sheaves
proof_stages.summary: A boundedness theorem with Srinivas supplies finitely many eventual Hilbert polynomials for the reflexive extensions j_*E_n of all bounded-rank F-divided bundles, proved by reducing to normal surfaces and controlling numerical Chern classes through blowups.

proof_stages.label: Bost Lefschetz theorem and curve surjectivity
proof_stages.summary: Bost's Lefschetz-type algebraization theorem for vector bundles on the formal completion along a smooth complete-intersection curve yields a surjection from the crystalline fundamental group of the curve to that of X.

proof_stages.label: Stable moduli reduction and Frobenius dynamics
proof_stages.summary: Using the curve surjectivity and the proper-case analysis on the curve, the argument reduces to stable numerically trivial bundles represented by moduli points on X-hat, whose Zariski closure carries a rational dominant Frobenius-pullback map.

proof_stages.label: Frobenius fixed point and trivialization
proof_stages.summary: A Frobenius fixed-point theorem over the algebraic closure of a finite field produces a Frobenius-periodic bundle, which is trivialized by a finite étale cover and hence is already trivial when π1^ét(X)=1, forcing the crystal itself to be trivial.

proof_stages.label: Reason for the Fpbar hypothesis
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains that the restriction to the algebraic closure of a finite field is currently needed because proper specialization controls the full étale fundamental group, whereas in the nonproper case only tame or prime-to-p specialization is available.

examples.name: Complex Malčev-Grothendieck regular-singular analogy
examples.note: The complex theorem that trivial étale fundamental group forbids nontrivial regular singular connections, via finite generation of the topological fundamental group, is used to motivate both the characteristic-p question and the boundedness substitute.
examples.engagement_reason: He invokes the complex Malčev–Grothendieck theorem as the historical regular-singular analogy and states its consequence, but does not work through a concrete example or derivation on it.

passes.reason: The complex Malčev–Grothendieck regular-singular theorem is used as a standard characteristic-zero analogue motivating the characteristic-p question, not as a refutation.
It is a general characteristic-zero theorem/analogy for regular-singular connections, used as motivation rather than as a structurally special object or a refutation.

examples.name: Irregular meromorphic connections after resolving turning points
examples.note: The final complex-analytic discussion of Malgrange-Kedlaya-Mochizuki lattices with fixed blowups and irregularities illustrates that analogous boundedness of Chern classes may persist beyond the regular singular setting.
examples.engagement_reason: He sets up irregular meromorphic connections with a fixed blow-up eliminating turning points, explains the local formal standard form after coverings, and reasons about the resulting lattices and Chern-class boundedness.

passes.reason: Irregular meromorphic connections with turning points resolved form a systematic class used to state a boundedness result, not a sporadic, extremal, symmetric, or adversarial example.
